flat_text: Hello. Hello. Hello? Hi, can you hear me? I can hear you. Can you hear me? Yeah, I can hear you now. Excellent. So you tried that and it didn't help. Okay. All right. Um, do you have any allergies to any medications? Uh, yeah. I'm allergic to clindamycin. And what happens when you take clindamycin? Um, I—I get some, like, a—a swelling, basically. Swelling. Okay. So it's quite—it seems like a quite severe allergy to it. Is that—is that right? Um, okay. Yeah. Fine. It's really—it's really interesting, uh, learning about—learning about how, uh, how the world works. Yeah. That's great. Do you have any aspirations for the future in terms of careers? Um, right. Excellent. So, um, at this stage, I would proceed to examination. But, um, based on your story, um, just to summarise, really, so in the last three days, you've been having a bit of burning pain on passing urine, a bit of crampy tummy pain lower down, um, essentially, but—but no other fevers or back pain, uh, if I—if I understand correctly. I think you probably do have what we call a simple UTI or a urinary tract infection, a bladder infection. Okay. Um, which can be readily treated. So I'll give you a course of antibiotics today, something. Okay. That's called either nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim. Uh, and it's normally a three-day course, uh, so one tablet twice a day for three days. And that should. Okay. Hopefully help your symptoms. But I also advise you to drink plenty of fluids. Let's try and flush that, you know, that infection out of your body, if we can. Um, you can try cranberry juice. That can sometimes help with your symptoms. Um, and if you're still not better by the end of this week, I want you to give me a call or come back and see me, and, uh, we can have another look at your symptoms. Okay? Okay. So three—three days of the medication, and then wait a little bit. If it still doesn't go away, call back? Yeah. Absolutely. Yeah. And things to look out for would be, you know, if you are having kind of high temperatures, back pain, um, just genuinely feeling more unwell. Uh, you know, I do want. Okay. To give me a call back, because then we do worry whether your infection may be spreading up towards your kidneys, which is something we want to avoid. So do—do give me a call if you have any concerns. Okay? Okay. Is there—is there anything, like, like, should I be worried about, like, like, not having sex or anything within the next week or anything like this? Yeah. I mean, if you can try and avoid it, I think that would be sensible. Um, uh, it's great that you're using barrier contraception. I would recommend you to continue using barrier contraception. But if you can avoid it while your symptoms clear up, that would probably be the best for you and your boyfriend. Okay. Okay. All right? Uh, that—that makes sense. Great. Um, okay. Thank you so much. You're all right. Wish you all the best. Yeah. Thank you. Take care. Bye. Bye.
